title: Kipp: Oracle-Gated, Placement-Invariant Paged Attention for Verifiable LLM Inference
description:
Kipp is a hand-written C11 inference engine for the pinned Qwen3 dense model family, with a scalar CPU correctness oracle and correctness-gated Apple Metal and NVIDIA CUDA backends. Its paged key-value cache is placement-invariant: the physical location of KV blocks is arbitrary, so a sequence's logits are bitwise-identical under any permutation of its block table. Kipp turns that property into a continuous correctness contract — a scramble gate that reverses a session's block table and requires bitwise-identical output — and a mutation study shows the gate catches a class of silent KV-addressing bugs that tolerance-based reference tests miss entirely.

This deposit is the software artifact accompanying the paper of the same title; every benchmark number in the paper is generated from committed result files under bench/results/.
